# API, UX, Data, Security, and Performance Checklist: Visualize Forecast Curves with Uncertainty Bands

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of UC-05 requirements for API, UX, data, security, and performance concerns before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/005-uc-05-visualize/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## API Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK001 Are all visualization API surfaces and their distinct responsibilities clearly specified for current-visualization retrieval and render-outcome reporting? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, FR-013; Plan §Implementation Steps 2, 8; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK002 Are response expectations defined for all relevant API outcome classes, including success, degraded visualization, fallback-shown, unavailable, unauthorized, forbidden, not found, and invalid request? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009-FR-013, FR-020; Plan §Implementation Steps 5, 8; Contract §ForecastVisualization, VisualizationRenderEvent]
- [x] CHK003 Is the boundary between visualization-data retrieval and client render reporting specified clearly enough that API consumers can distinguish server assembly outcomes from client render outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013; Plan §Implementation Steps 3, 5, 8; Data Model §VisualizationLoadRecord; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK004 Are category-filter and forecast-product selection requirements explicit enough to avoid ambiguity about which query inputs the visualization API must accept or reject? [Gap, Plan §Summary; Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary; Contract §/api/v1/forecast-visualizations/current]

## UX Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK005 Are the dashboard-view requirements complete enough to define the required visible elements beyond the chart itself, including category filtering, alerts/status visibility, and last-updated metadata? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001-FR-008; Plan §Constraints; Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary]
- [x] CHK006 Are the visual distinction requirements for historical demand, forecast curves, and `P10`/`P50`/`P90` uncertainty bands specific enough to be objectively interpreted by implementers and reviewers? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005-FR-008, FR-014-FR-018; Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK007 Are degraded, unavailable, and fallback states described consistently enough that the user experience for missing history, missing uncertainty, stale fallback, and render failure is not open to multiple interpretations? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2; Spec §User Story 3; Spec §FR-009-FR-014, FR-020; Plan §Implementation Step 5]
- [x] CHK008 Are user-facing boundary cues defined clearly enough that reviewers can tell how the dashboard should communicate where historical demand ends and forecast demand begins? [Measurability, Spec §FR-015; Spec §SC-007; Data Model §ForecastVisualization]

## Data Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK009 Are reused entities from UC-01 through UC-04 identified clearly enough that UC-05 does not accidentally redefine forecast lineage already covered by earlier specs? [Consistency, Plan §Summary; Data Model §Reused Entities From Previous Specs]
- [x] CHK010 Are the visualization-specific entities and their relationships defined clearly enough to distinguish visualization load records, fallback snapshots, and derived view models from daily and weekly forecast entities? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities; Data Model §VisualizationLoadRecord; Data Model §VisualizationSnapshot; Data Model §ForecastVisualization]
- [x] CHK011 Are identity, uniqueness, and exclusivity rules specified for source forecast references, selected forecast products, and fallback snapshot eligibility? [Clarity, Data Model §VisualizationLoadRecord Validation Rules; Data Model §VisualizationSnapshot Validation Rules; Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [x] CHK012 Are the data-window requirements explicit enough to remove ambiguity about the previous 7-day historical context, forecast boundary, and 24-hour fallback expiration rule? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-019, FR-020; Data Model §VisualizationLoadRecord; Data Model §VisualizationSnapshot]

## Security Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK013 Are protected visualization resources identified consistently across the specification, plan, data model, and contract surfaces? [Consistency, Plan §Constitution Check; Plan §Implementation Step 2; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK014 Are requirements explicit about keeping all authentication and authorization enforcement in backend contracts rather than in frontend-only logic? [Clarity, Plan §Constitution Check; Plan §Implementation Steps 2, 7; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK015 Are data-protection requirements specified clearly enough to prohibit raw source rows, feature matrices, secrets, or model internals from appearing in visualization payloads, render-event reports, or logs? [Completeness, Plan §Constraints; Plan §Implementation Step 8; Contract §summary]
- [x] CHK016 Is the separation between access-control failures and visualization business outcomes defined clearly enough to avoid mixing unauthorized or forbidden cases with degraded, fallback, or unavailable dashboard states? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013; Plan §Implementation Step 2; Data Model §VisualizationLoadRecord; Contract §Paths]

## Performance Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK017 Are performance targets quantified for the primary visualization flow rather than described only in broad terms such as "fast" or "responsive"? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001; Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK018 Are measurable performance expectations defined for alternate visualization outcomes such as degraded history-missing paths, uncertainty-missing paths, and fallback-shown paths, or explicitly scoped out? [Gap, Spec §SC-003-SC-005; Plan §Implementation Step 5]
- [x] CHK019 Are the requirements explicit about which timing expectations apply to successful dashboard loads versus terminal load recording and render-failure reporting? [Consistency, Spec §SC-001, SC-005, SC-006; Plan §Performance Goals; Data Model §VisualizationLoadRecord]
- [x] CHK020 Are scale and concurrency assumptions for repeated dashboard opens, per-category filtering, and dual forecast-product support documented or intentionally excluded? [Gap, Plan §Scale/Scope; Plan §Implementation Steps 1, 6, 7]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
